summary: Dominic Flores, IT Director at Silverline Brands, called Aegis Cloud Security support regarding a two-week-old open case about backup jobs significantly overrunning their scheduled 1 AM–5 AM window, with SQL Server backups now taking up to 8–9 hours and causing production performance degradation. Support engineer David Kim identified a dramatic throughput drop from ~800 MB/s to ~180–220 MB/s, potentially linked to backend network routing changes made during a March remediation effort for a separate module (Aegis Detect). David initiated immediate workarounds including a manual agent update to version 4.2.0 and SQL job priority adjustments, and escalated the issue as P1 to the infrastructure team. A follow-up call was scheduled for the next morning at 10 AM Central with a Protect platform engineer to review root cause findings.
actionItems: David Kim: Escalate case to Protect infrastructure team as P1 and investigate backend network routing changes from March remediation as root cause of throughput degradation
David Kim: Deploy agent update 4.2.0 to Silverline Brands environment manually within the hour and adjust SQL backup job priority settings for tonight's run
David Kim: Send calendar invite and case update email to Dominic within 30 minutes for 10 AM Central follow-up call with a Protect platform engineer
David Kim: Set up alert to be paged directly if SQL backup job duration exceeds six hours during tonight's backup window

keyMoments.text: Dominic expresses frustration about slow initial response time despite paying for premium support
keyMoments.speaker: Dominic Flores

keyMoments.text: David identifies throughput dropped from ~800 MB/s to ~180-220 MB/s, confirming a significant platform-side issue
keyMoments.speaker: David Kim

keyMoments.text: Dominic alarmed to learn a separate module incident (Aegis Detect) may have broken backups with no proactive customer notification
keyMoments.speaker: Dominic Flores

keyMoments.text: Dominic acknowledges appreciation for David's transparency and commits to tomorrow's follow-up call, signaling cautious de-escalation
keyMoments.speaker: Dominic Flores
